overview.description: Cybersecurity research group at CTU Prague specialising in machine learning for network intrusion detection, malware analysis, and civil society protection
overview.description_long: The Stratosphere Research Laboratory is the cybersecurity group at the Artificial Intelligence Centre, Faculty of Electrical Engineering, Czech Technical University in Prague. Founded in 2013 by Sebastian García as a continuation of his PhD work, the lab focuses on applying machine learning to network security problems. Key contributions include Slips — a free-software ML-based behavioural intrusion detection/prevention system — as well as widely-used public datasets (CTU-13, IoT-23, Hornet), the NetSecGame framework for training AI security agents, and the Civilsphere project protecting NGOs and at-risk individuals from targeted cyber attacks. The lab is funded by the NLnet Foundation and operates the joint AIC/Avast AI and Cybersecurity Laboratory.
overview.tagline: Machine Learning, Cybersecurity and Helping Others

history.origin_story: Founded at CTU Prague as a continuation of Sebastian García's PhD research in machine-learning-based network intrusion detection; grew into a multi-national team of ~20 researchers combining open-source tool development with academic publication.

key_milestones.event: Founded at Czech Technical University in Prague by Sebastian García
Released CTU-13 dataset — widely cited labeled botnet/malware network traffic dataset
Launched joint AIC/Avast AI and Cybersecurity Laboratory; Sebastian García appointed to Avast Chair
Selected as first Czech organisation accepted as Google Summer of Code mentor organisation
Accepted for Google Summer of Code 2024 for second consecutive year; released NetSecGame framework for AI security agents

leadership.name: Sebastian Garcia
leadership.role: Founder & Head of Cybersecurity Group / PI

awards: Google Summer of Code mentor organisation 2023 (first Czech org selected)
Google Summer of Code mentor organisation 2024

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: Academic research group; not a commercial product. Their work on ML for cybersecurity and defensive LLMs is complementary to, rather than displaced by, foundation models.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Research lab embedded in CTU Prague with grant funding from NLnet Foundation and an industry partnership with Avast; market risk does not apply in the commercial sense.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: Sustained by university institutional support, NLnet Foundation grants, and the Avast Chair endowment; typical academic funding model with no commercial runway dependency.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Research group within a public university; commercial risk categories are not applicable. The lab has stable multi-source funding and a strong international publication record.

metadata.notes: Sebastian García has 3,747+ Google Scholar citations as of early 2026. Lab is ~20 researchers. First Czech org accepted as Google Summer of Code mentor (2023). NLnet Foundation is primary external funder.
